zh.lifeSummary: 當William Le Fleming於1059年出生在英國英格蘭波藍德時，他的父親Michael Fleming19歲，母親Beatrix de Beaumont20歲。他於1085年之前與Baroness Ada Florennes在英國結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1150年在威爾士凱爾納方史爾凱爾納福 卡素享年91歲，埋葬在英國波藍德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William Le Fleming于1059年出生在英国英格兰波藍德时，他的父亲Michael Fleming19岁，母亲Beatrix de Beaumont20岁。他于1085年之前与Baroness Ada Florennes在英国结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于1150年在威尔士凱爾納方史爾凱爾納福 卡素享年91岁，埋葬在英国波藍德。

nameMeanings.name: Le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Fleming
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: ethnic name for someone from Flanders, from Middle English fleming. The word reflects a Norman French form of Old French flamanc ‘Fleming’, from the stem flam- + the ancient Germanic suffix -ing. In the Middle Ages there was considerable commercial intercourse between England and the Netherlands, particularly in the wool trade, and many Flemish weavers and dyers settled in England. This surname is also common in south and east Scotland and in Ireland, where it is sometimes found in the Gaelicized form Pléamonn.
German: variant of   Flemming  , cognate with 1 above.

variants.name: Flemings
meanings: English: plural form of   Fleming  .
Americanized form of Flemish Vleminckx: patronymic from Vleminck, an ethnic name for someone from Flanders, Middle Dutch vleminc.

variants.name: Flanders
meanings: English: ethnic name from Middle English Fla(u)ndres ‘Flanders’, denoting someone from Flanders, a historical region covering parts of modern-day Belgium, France, and the Netherlands. Compare Fleming .

variants.name: Fleeman
meanings: English: variant of   Fleming  .

variants.name: Flemming
meanings: German: ethnic name for someone from Flanders, Middle High German vlaeminc. People from Flanders spread throughout Germany, as well as England, in the Middle Ages as clothmakers and dyers.
English: variant of   Fleming  .

variants.name: Flamenco
meanings: Spanish: ethnic name from flamenco ‘Fleming, Flemish person’ (see Fleming ).

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Battle of Hastings
historicEvents.description: Battle of Hastings was fought between the Norman-French army and the battle-wary English army on October 14, 1066. Duke William of Normandy believed he was the rightful king rather than Harold, who had been crowned king several months earlier. Harold was killed in battle and William was crowned king on Christmas Day 1066.

historicEvents.title: Domesday Book
historicEvents.description: Domesday Book is Britain's earliest extant public record with information containing land surveys and landholdings commissioned by William I. The book was completed in 1086.

historicEvents.title: The Crusades
historicEvents.description: A series of religious wars between Christians and Muslims, known as the Crusades, occurred between 1096 and 1291, and played a large role in European Christian dominance in the Middle East.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: When William Le Fleming was born in 1059, in Cumberland, England, United Kingdom, his father, Michael Fleming, was 19 and his mother, Beatrix de Beaumont, was 20. He married Baroness Ada Florennes before 1085, in England. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died in 1150, in Caernarvon Castle, Caernarfonshire, Wales, at the age of 91, and was buried in Cumberland, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William Le Fleming est né en 1059, à Cumberland, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Michael Fleming, avait 19 ans et sa mère, Beatrix de Beaumont, avait 20 ans. Il a épousé Baroness Ada Florennes avant 1085, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé en 1150, à Caernarvon Castle, Caernarfonshire, Galles, à l’âge de 91 ans, et a été enterré à Cumberland, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als William Le Fleming 1059 in Cumberland, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Michael Fleming 19 und seine Mutter Beatrix de Beaumont 20. Er hat vor 1085 in England Baroness Ada Florennes geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1150 im Alter von 91 Jahren in Caernarvon Castle, Caernarfonshire, Wales und wurde in Cumberland, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando William Le Fleming è nato il nel 1059 nel Cumberland, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Michael Fleming, aveva 19 anni e il suo madre, Beatrix de Beaumont, aveva 20 anni. Lui sposato Baroness Ada Florennes prima del 1085, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel 1150, nel Caernarvon Castle, Caernarfonshire, Galles, all'età di 91 anni, e fu sepolto a Cumberland, England.

ja.lifeSummary: William Le Flemingが1059年にイギリスイギリスクウンバーランドで生まれた時，父Michael Flemingは19歳，母Beatrix de Beaumontは20歳であった。1085年以前にイギリスでBaroness Ada Florennesと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼は1150年にウェールズケエルナルフォンシュアイアケエルナルボン カストルで91歳で死去し，イギリスクウンバーランドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: William Le Fleming(이)가 1059년 영국 잉글랜드 쿠벌랜드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Michael Fleming(은)는 19세였고, 그의 어머니 Beatrix de Beaumont(은)는 20세였다. 그는 1085년 이전 영국에서 Baroness Ada Florennes(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1150년 웨일스 카이른아르폰실르 카이른아르폰 카스틀에서 91세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 쿠벌랜드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William Le Fleming nasceu em 1059, em Cumberland, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Michael Fleming, tinha 19 anos e sua mãe, Beatrix de Beaumont, tinha 20 anos. Ele casou-se com Baroness Ada Florennes antes 1085, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em 1150, em Caernarvon Castle, Caernarfonshire, Gales, com 91 anos, e foi sepultado em Cumberland, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William Le Fleming родился в 1059 г. в местности кумбирланд, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Michael Fleming было 19 лет, а матери, которую звали Beatrix de Beaumont, 20. Он женился на женщине по имени Baroness Ada Florennes перед 1085 г. в местности Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер в 1150 г. в местности каирнарвон кастл, каирнарфоншир, Уэльс в возрасте 91 год и был похоронен в местности кумбирланд, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando William Le Fleming nació en 1059, en Cumberland, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Michael Fleming, tenía 19 años y su mamá, Beatrix de Beaumont, tenía 20 años. Se casó con Baroness Ada Florennes antes de 1085, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en 1150, en Caernarvon Castle, Caernarfonshire, Gales, a la edad de 91 años, y fue sepultado en Cumberland, Inglaterra.
